scanner_blind_spots.reason: Buyback announcements require qualitative interpretation of management conviction — the scanner surfaces mechanical buyback volume but missed CEO explicitly stating stock 'significantly undervalues future prospects' alongside $4B total authorization (18% of float). Two discovery experts (homer + insider_flow) independently flagged this as HIGH conviction.

Leidos classified as defense IT services in scanner's sector taxonomy — missed the $2.7B Army hypersonic weapons contract (May 12) that fundamentally repositions it as a weapons systems prime. Contract hit same day as assessment and requires real-time defense contracting monitoring unavailable to automated screeners.

Pharma royalty business economics structurally mismodeled by automated screeners — Halozyme's gross D/E of 991x triggers debt-screening filters, masking that net leverage is only 2.5x (declining to 1.2x by year-end) and the business generates royalty FCF growing 30-35% annually. Scanner found it but scored it incorrectly due to gross leverage signal.

Scanner lagged the Q1 2026 earnings reaction — the triple beat (EPS +22%, revenue +8.5%, adj. EBITDA +18%) with 631M MAU represents a rerating trigger the automated system hadn't processed, resulting in a scanner price of $18.81 that was already stale. Discovery pipeline identified the post-earnings opportunity independently.

Post-earnings 14-18% stock decline on margin concerns caused the scanner to de-prioritize Arista despite a fundamental beat and guidance raise. Discovery found the Raymond James upgrade (May 14) + CFO clarification on margins 9 days post-earnings + 'best demand environment in Arista history' statement — qualitative signals the scanner cannot process.

Nuclear power utilities with multi-decade power purchase agreements fall outside scanner's standard financial screening parameters — negative FCF of -$4.5B during capital deployment phase disqualifies it from value screens despite three signed AI hyperscaler contracts (Microsoft, Meta, CyrusOne) validating a unique scarcity asset.

Saskatchewan flooding (May 11) removed 1.5M lbs/month from global uranium supply — a real-time geological event requiring current news monitoring to catch. No automated financial screener can detect mine output disruption from a Canadian river flooding event. Entry window created by supply shock layered on top of pre-existing structural uranium deficit.

High trailing P/E (79x) disqualified Vertiv from scanner's value screens, missing the exceptional forward picture — FY2026 adj EPS guidance of $6.35 represents 51% YoY growth with $15B backlog (+109% YoY). The scanner optimizes for current multiples, not forward visibility driven by an enormous order book in a capital-constrained liquid cooling market.
